scenario_title: Add CNAME and add address data at the same owner
test_rationale: The test starts with a clean A RRset at cname1.campus.edu. and then sends one UPDATE that adds both a CNAME and an A RR to the same owner. This creates a semantic conflict that RFC 2181 forbids but RFC 2136 does not resolve operationally within one message. The follow-up CNAME and A queries make it possible to distinguish servers that reject the update, preserve only the CNAME, or keep the A data.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE as NOERROR and keeps only the newly added CNAME, removing or suppressing the A data to preserve CNAME exclusivity.
group_1.post_state: cname1.campus.edu. has CNAME target.campus.edu.; A RRset absent.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE with YXRRSET when the added CNAME conflicts with existing or concurrently added address data at the same owner name.
group_2.post_state: cname1.campus.edu. remains with its original A RRset; no CNAME present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 and §10.3 require CNAME exclusivity, but RFC 2136 §3.4.2 does not define the required server behavior when a single UPDATE introduces both a CNAME and other data at the same owner name. As a result, implementations may reject the UPDATE, accept only one RRset, or enforce exclusivity by deleting conflicting data, producing different replies and post-update RRsets.
